_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Multi-state batch (UT/WA/AK/AL). CURRENCY FIXES: ty-jensen LOST the 4/25/2026 UT GOP convention (1.4%, last; Mike Kennedy won outright 78.7%, no June primary) -> status lost. wesley-meier is actually a REPUBLICAN (FEC H6WA04182), not a Democrat, and did NOT file with WA SoS during filing week -> absent from the Aug 4 ballot -> party corrected + status former/not_running. UPGRADE: jonny-larsen prior roster_verified -> evidence_federal; campaign-site runtime JSON yielded a full platform; he WON the 4/25 UT Dem convention (94.9%) and is the NOMINEE (no June primary). FULL SCORES: mike-dunleavy (AK Gov, term-limited, ends Dec 2026) and steve-marshall (AL AG sitting; LOST 5/19 R Senate primary but remains AG through Jan 2027) scored from documented official records.

set.office: U.S. Representative UT-04 (2026 R candidate — LOST 4/25 GOP convention, 1.4%, last; Mike Kennedy won the nomination outright at 78.7%, so no June 23 primary)

profile.background: Tyrone 'Ty' Jensen — small-business owner and podcaster from Richfield, UT; perennial candidate (2018 write-in U.S. Senate, 2020 vs. Rep. Chris Stewart, 2023 special election). 2026 Republican candidate for the open UT-04 seat (Rep. Burgess Owens not seeking reelection) who ran the convention-only route and was eliminated at the April 25, 2026 Utah GOP convention.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Candidacy-currency fix: LOST at convention (1.4%, 13 delegate votes, last of 5); Mike Kennedy cleared the 60% threshold (78.7%) so there is no UT-04 GOP primary and Jensen is off the ballot. Policy footprint was nearly empty: his campaign Issues/Policy pages were placeholders never populated; the only HARD own-words position found is opposing 'drag shows in schools' (Instagram). Everything else NO EVIDENCE FOUND — scored on that single documented cell only.

ty-jensen.notes_set: LOST the April 25, 2026 Utah GOP convention (1.4%, last place); Kennedy won the nomination outright (78.7%), no June 23 primary. Status set to lost. Stated process-reform priorities (anti-omnibus/single-item bills, ban congressional insider trading, term limits, Epstein files) and 'keep drag shows out of schools' (Instagram, ~Apr 2026). No findable position on abortion, marriage definition, guns, elections, immigration, or religious liberty.

4.note: Instagram (@jensen4utah, ~Apr 2026): 'Committed to keeping Drag Shows OUT of schools' — opposes promotion of LGBTQ identity in public schools. His own words.

set.office: U.S. Representative WA-04 (filed FEC 3/2025 as a Republican but did NOT file with the WA Secretary of State during filing week; absent from the Aug 4, 2026 top-two primary ballot — not an active candidate)

profile.background: Wesley Meier — Richland, WA. Filed an FEC Statement of Candidacy (H6WA04182) on March 16, 2025 as a Republican for the open WA-04 seat (Rep. Dan Newhouse retiring), but did not file with the Washington Secretary of State during the May 2026 filing week and is absent from the confirmed 11-candidate August 4, 2026 top-two primary field.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Two fixes: (1) PARTY CORRECTED D->R per FEC H6WA04182 (the WA-04 Democrat is John Duresky, a different person); (2) CURRENCY — not on the Aug 4, 2026 ballot per BallotReady + Spokesman-Review (June 2026) candidate roundups; treated as withdrawn/not-running. No campaign site, social, questionnaire, or any policy statement located — nothing to score (roster_verified, all cells null).

wesley-meier.notes_set: Party corrected from D to R (FEC candidate record H6WA04182 lists Republican Party; the WA-04 Democrat is John Duresky). Candidacy-currency fix: filed FEC in March 2025 but did NOT file with the WA Secretary of State during filing week, so he is absent from the confirmed August 4, 2026 WA-04 top-two primary ballot — marked not_running / former. No documented issue positions found.

set.office: U.S. Representative UT-04 (2026 D NOMINEE · open seat — Owens not seeking reelection; won 4/25 Dem convention 94.9%, no June primary; faces R nominee Mike Kennedy 11/3 in an R+14 district)

profile.background: Jonny (Jonathan) Larsen — USMC infantry combat veteran (two Iraq tours, 2003-2004), healthcare-systems administrator; 2026 Democratic NOMINEE for UT-04 (open after Rep. Burgess Owens declined reelection). Won the April 25, 2026 Utah Democratic convention with 94.9%, advancing straight to the November general election (no June 23 primary).
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Upgraded from roster_verified to evidence_federal. A prior pass found his site was a JS-only SPA; this pass located the app's runtime JSON content files (jonnyutah4congress.com/data/the-commitment.json, about-jonny.json, press-releases.json) which carry his full platform in his own words (HARD). Scored from those + an ABC4 quote. A conventional populist-labor Democrat (pro-choice, pro-LGBTQ, anti-voucher) with notable CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: an antiwar/Article-I war-powers constitutionalist and small-farm/anti-data-center advocate. Also anti-crypto. Silent on 2A (notable for a combat vet), religious liberty, and election procedure -> null.

jonny-larsen.notes_set: Candidacy-currency: WON the April 25, 2026 UT Democratic convention (94.9%) — he is the NOMINEE, not a primary contestant; no June 23 primary; faces GOP nominee Mike Kennedy in the Nov. 3, 2026 general (R+14). Platform (own words, campaign runtime JSON): pro-choice ('between a woman and her doctor'); pro-LGBTQ/transgender protections (2025 Pride parade); opposes private-school vouchers / pro-public-school funding; comprehensive immigration reform with 'workable pathways' and anti-ICE-raid; anti-crypto ('needs heavy regulation'); anti-Citizens-United, ban congressional stock trading. DEVIATIONS: condemned the U.S.-Israel strike on Iran as unconstitutional 'war without the approval of Congress' (Article I); active small-farm/water advocate (protested the Joule Capital data-center pipeline before the Millard County Commission).

0.note: Campaign platform: women deserve 'full control over their own healthcare decisions... between a woman and her doctor. Not a politician.' — pro-choice, contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

2.note: Platform: 'LGBTQ+ and transgender Utahns deserve the same protections everyone else has'; pictured at the 2025 Pride parade — affirms, does not reject, transgender ideology.

4.note: Platform: 'No child should walk into school feeling unsafe because of who they are' — supports LGBTQ inclusion in schools, contrary to opposing its promotion.

0.note: Platform (Education): opposes diverting 'public school funding to private voucher programs'; 'I will fight to protect public school funding' — contrary to universal school choice/vouchers.

Bio: got into blockchain, 'saw it scamming people out of their savings, and walked away. That industry needs heavy regulation.' — anti-decentralized-crypto, contrary to supporting crypto as legal tender.

1.note: Platform: 'real reform that secures the border, creates workable pathways'; 'We do not need masked agents terrorizing communities' (anti-ICE-raid) — contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

0.note: On the U.S.-Israel strike on Iran (2/28/2026): 'Today he violated the Constitution and took this country into war without the approval of Congress.' Directly affirms the Article I congressional war-powers requirement. Cross-party deviation.

3.note: Active small-farm advocate: protested the Joule Capital 12-GW data-center gas pipeline before the Millard County Commission (June 2026); 'investing in farmers over billionaires'; opposes 'use it or lose it' water laws — small-farm/land protection.

set.office: Governor of Alaska (term-limited — second consecutive term ends Dec 7, 2026; not seeking reelection)

profile.background: Mike Dunleavy — Republican Governor of Alaska since December 2018 (re-elected 2022). Former state senator and educator. Term-limited under the Alaska Constitution (no third consecutive term); his term ends December 7, 2026 and he is not on the 2026 gubernatorial ballot.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Scored from documented official actions (bills signed/vetoed, proclamations, executive/board actions, multistate litigation, NRA rating). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION flagged: openly pro-MORE-(legal)-immigration ('we need more immigrants... a strong wall with many doors') — softer than typical R border rhetoric; left border cells null rather than score a FALSE without a rubric-specific contrary statement. His pro-life court-funding vetoes were ruled unconstitutional and reversed (a court-forced retreat). RCV-repeal support is real but maps to no specific election-integrity rubric cell -> election cells null.

mike-dunleavy.notes_set: Sitting AK Governor, term-limited (ends Dec 7, 2026), not seeking reelection (candidacy_status corrected from 'incumbent_seeking_reelection'). Documented record: pro-life ('most pro-life state' goal; Right to Life Day proclamations; vetoed abortion-related court funding [ruled unconstitutional, reversed]; vetoed a 2024 contraception-access measure); pro-women's-sports/anti-trans-in-girls'-sports; parental-rights bill (consent for sex-ed and name/pronoun changes); strong correspondence/homeschool & school-choice defender; signed porn age-verification (HB254); Day of Prayer + kept churches open during COVID; joined the 19-governor anti-ESG alliance; major budget/PFD spending vetoes; signed HB49 repealing SB91 (tough-on-crime); 'Statehood Defense' litigation vs. federal overreach (ANWR, vaccine mandates); authorized National Guard to the southern border; NRA 'A'-rated, signed HB61 (gun-store protection). DEVIATION: favors more legal immigration ('many doors').

0.note: Stated goal to make Alaska 'the most pro-life state in the entire country' (2023 State of the State); annual Right to Life Day proclamations 'committed to defending unborn Alaskans'; vetoed a 2024 contraception-access measure.

2.note: Publicly urged a law barring biological males from female sports ('nullifies the fair nature of competitive sports'); his appointed State Board of Education voted to limit transgender girls in girls' sports — affirms biological sex.

0.note: Strong defender of Alaska's correspondence/homeschool allotment program (~$4,500/student) and school choice; vetoed SB140 partly for lacking charter-school expansion.

1.note: Introduced a parental-rights bill requiring parental permission for sex-ed and for name/pronoun changes in school, and barring schools from withholding student info from parents.

2.note: Same parental-rights bill bans sex-ed before 4th grade and restricts gender/pronoun changes in schools — opposes gender-ideology/sex-ed content in K-12.

3.note: Supported HB254 requiring age-verification software for pornographic content accessed by minors.

0.note: Declared an Alaska Day of Prayer and Hope and worked with churches to keep religious services operating during COVID — affirms public profession of faith.

3.note: Personally read a proclamation and prayer and hosted Christian leaders for a public Day of Prayer — supports prayer in the public square.

2.note: Fiscal hawk: vetoed ~$444M from the FY20 operating budget pushing spending restraint; repeated large budget vetoes — opposes deficit/over-spending.

4.note: Joined the 19-governor anti-ESG alliance (March 2023) pledging to leverage state funds against ESG asset managers; pushed to cut state ties to banks refusing Arctic oil/gas financing.

0.note: Campaigned on a 'war on criminals'; signed HB49 repealing the SB91 reform — affirmatively backs law enforcement and tougher crime policy.

1.note: HB49 tightened 'catch and release' bail and restored drug possession to a felony — opposes soft-on-crime/lenient-bail policy.

2.note: HB49 stiffened felony sentencing and restored judicial discretion — proportional punishment of offenders.

1.note: Joined multistate suits against Biden's OSHA and CMS COVID vaccine mandates ('I am not anti-vaccine; I am anti-mandate') — declined unconstitutional federal mandates.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach across guns/health/land — vaccine-mandate suits + ANWR/land litigation.

3.note: 'Statehood Defense' initiative: dedicated state funding for lawsuits asserting Alaska sovereignty against federal overreach (ANILCA/Statehood Act).

4.note: Sued the federal government over ANWR oil/gas lease restrictions and federal control of Alaska land/resources — opposed federal capture of state land.

0.note: Authorized Alaska National Guard deployment to support the federal Southwest Border Mission and backed Texas's border effort — active military border presence. (Note: separately favors more legal immigration — see notes.)
Champions Alaska's permitless/constitutional carry and 2A-sanctuary status; NRA-PVF endorsed — supports constitutional carry.

1.note: NRA 'A' rating; signed HB61 barring officials from closing gun stores during disasters — opposes gun-control/restriction measures.

set.office: Attorney General of Alabama (sitting; term-limited, ends Jan 2027) — lost a 2026 federal primary bid 5/19, remains AG

profile.background: Steve Marshall — Republican Attorney General of Alabama since 2017; former chairman of the Republican Attorneys General Association (RAGA). Ran for the open 2026 U.S. Senate seat (Tuberville's) and LOST the May 19, 2026 Republican primary (3rd, ~25%, conceded 5/20). Remains the sitting AG (term-limited as AG; term runs through January 2027).
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Scored as the SITTING AG from documented official legal actions (amicus briefs, multistate lawsuits, enforcement, official statements). Aggressively conservative across all ten areas. One deviation noted: after the LePage 'embryos are children' ruling, Marshall publicly committed NOT to prosecute IVF families/providers — prosecutorial discretion that limited the reach of a pro-life ruling (left sanctity q2 null, not scored either way). Several signature fights were judicial LOSSES (Allen v. Milligan; out-of-state-abortion-travel threat ruled unconstitutional) — positions scored, outcomes noted.

steve-marshall.notes_set: Currency: LOST the May 19, 2026 R U.S. Senate primary (3rd, ~25%, ~118k votes; Moore + Hudson to the 6/16 runoff; conceded 5/20). REMAINS sitting Alabama AG through Jan 2027 — scored as AG. Documented record: defended AL's abortion ban + threatened prosecution of out-of-state abortion travel (ruled unconstitutional); won Boe v. Marshall defending the minor-gender-medicine ban + Skrmetti amicus + women's-sports briefs; Title IX 'redefine sex' suit; St. Isidore religious-charter-school amicus; 303 Creative + Pensacola-cross religious-liberty briefs; major anti-ESG campaign (BlackRock letter, proxy-advisor inquiry, Big Three antitrust suit, $29.5M settlement); defended GA SB202 + Eakin election-federalism amicus; nitrogen-hypoxia execution of Kenneth Smith (death-penalty enforcement); nationwide injunction vs. Biden contractor vaccine mandate + EPA/Title IX suits as RAGA chair; 22-state anti-sanctuary brief; ATF pistol-brace suit + firearm-accessory-ban amicus. DEVIATION: declined to prosecute IVF families post-LePage.

0.note: Aggressively defends Alabama's near-total abortion ban; asserted authority to prosecute groups facilitating out-of-state abortion travel (ruled unconstitutional 2024) and in 2025 sought a crackdown on out-of-state 'shield law' abortion-pill providers — strongly pro-life posture.

2.note: Won Boe v. Marshall defending Alabama's ban on transgender meds for minors; filed amicus backing TN in U.S. v. Skrmetti ('a really good day for kids'); led multistate women's-sports briefs — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Sued to block the Biden Title IX rule redefining sex as gender identity in schools; led girls'-sports coalitions — opposes LGBTQ-ideology promotion in schools/policy.

0.note: Filed amicus urging SCOTUS to allow religious (Catholic) charter schools (St. Isidore) — supports school choice.

1.note: Defended Alabama's SB184/VCAP law barring gender-transition procedures for minors — supports parental control over minors' gender interventions.

2.note: Title IX 'redefine sex' suit + minor-transition defense — opposes gender-ideology in K-12 schools.

1.note: Joined the amicus in 303 Creative v. Elenis (web designer) and defended religious preschools/Amish schools — supports conscience exemptions for Christian business owners and religious organizations.

2.note: Praised the 303 Creative ruling: 'the government cannot coerce anyone to speak against their deeply held beliefs' — opposes compelled speech against conviction.

3.note: Led a 13-state amicus supporting Pensacola's right to display a historic cross in a public park — supports Christian symbols in the public square.

4.note: Extensive anti-ESG record: 21-AG BlackRock letter, proxy-advisor (ISS/Glass Lewis) inquiry, antitrust suit against BlackRock/State Street/Vanguard ($29.5M settlement) — opposes ESG capture + anti-trust on financial cartels.

3.note: Filed amicus defending Georgia's SB202 (which limited drop boxes/ballot harvesting and tightened absentee rules) and the Eakin brief upholding PA's ballot-date requirement — opposes loose mail-in/drop-box/harvesting practices.

0.note: RAGA chair with a consistently pro-law-enforcement record; aggressively pursued capital justice (Kenneth Smith execution) — affirmatively backs law enforcement.

2.note: Sought and carried out the execution of a convicted murderer — proportional punishment of evildoers and justice for victims.

4.note: Vigorously defended and carried out capital punishment (first nitrogen-hypoxia execution) — affirms the magistrate's God-ordained duty to bear the sword.

1.note: Won a nationwide injunction against Biden's federal-contractor COVID vaccine mandate; sued over Head Start and healthcare-worker mandates — declined unconstitutional federal mandates.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on education/sex (Title IX), guns (ATF pistol-brace suit), and life/religious liberty — multistate challenges.

3.note: As RAGA chair, led/joined numerous multistate suits asserting state sovereignty against unconstitutional federal action.

4.note: Eakin amicus opposing federal courts striking down state election laws ('that's a power grab') — opposed federal capture of state elections.

2.note: Joined a 22-state brief opposing Illinois/Chicago sanctuary laws, supported Florida's sanctuary-city ban, and backed enforcement against sanctuary cities — opposes sanctuary policies.

1.note: Joined a 25-state suit (with the NRA) against the ATF pistol-brace rule and a SCOTUS amicus against a federal firearm-accessory ban — opposes federal firearms restrictions.

4.note: Sued the ATF over the pistol-brace rule ('weaponizing the administrative state') — directly opposes ATF overreach.
